title: ranunculus fallax subsp. altior
intro: Ranunculus fallax subsp. altior, also known as the greater creeping buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus fallax subsp. altior include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin issues and injuries. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to use caution and consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a medicinal remedy.
